Empires & Puzzles Leonie Review: The Nine-Lives Survivor

Leonie brings a unique survival mechanic to Empires & Puzzles with her Nine Lives passive. But does her Fast Hit-3 and Surge Bleed damage hold up in the current meta? We break down her stats, skills, and best use cases in this comprehensive guide.
Leonie Review: Is 9 Lives Enough for the Meta?
Leonie: The Purr-sistent Plague of the Jungle
Leonie arrived with the Empires & Puzzles 8th Birthday celebration, introducing the

family to the game. As a Fast speed Nature Rogue, she is designed to be a thorn in the side of minion-heavy teams and a persistent nuisance that simply refuses to stay dead. While she lacks the raw, screen-clearing power of some modern nukers, her unique mechanics allow her to outlast opponents in a battle of attrition.

She is essentially a "harasser" archetype—fast enough to fire often, annoying enough to disrupt synergy with defense downs and bleeds, and incredibly difficult to remove permanently thanks to her family bonus and passive.

Leonie features a striking, feral design that leans heavily into the "Wild Cat" theme. She is an anthropomorphic lioness warrior, decked out in tribal jungle gear with lethal claw weapons. The art style is vibrant, capturing a sense of agility and predatory danger. Compared to the more mystical or armored designs of recent sets (like Astral Elves or Constructs), she feels grounded and physical.
Leonie's stat distribution is heavily skewed towards
, which is exceptional and still competitive with heroes released nearly a year later. However, her
are beginning to show their age compared to the latest 1260+ Power creep monsters, who often sport 1300+ Defense and 2300+ Health.

She is a classic "glass cannon" build. In the current meta, she can be fragile if caught without her dodge or revive active. You will need to rely on her Rogue talent and passives to keep her in the fight, as she won't soak up much direct punishment.
Special Skill: Claws of the Lioness
Leonie's special is a multi-layered tool that requires understanding to maximize.

If she fires again within 6 turns (very likely at Fast speed), the bleed refreshes and increases by 300 total damage. This "ramping" mechanic forces the opponent to cleanse or eventually melt.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Wild Cat family and Leonie's passives are arguably more impactful than her active skill.

The Dodge chance (up to 15% for 3 heroes) is a nice survival layer, but even as a solo hero (5%), it adds a layer of RNG that can ruin an attacker's day, especially combined with her Rogue class dodge.
This is the headline feature. An
is statistically absurd. While the chance decreases with each use, the fact that she comes back with
is terrifying. She often wakes up, slashes your team, applies bleed, and dies again, only to repeat the cycle. It cannot be blocked, making her immune to anti-revive tech like Kravekrush.
She is immune to Defense modifications.
She ignores Defense Down (Ramming Pulverizer, etc.) and Wither. She is consistently sturdy.
She cannot benefit from Defense Up (Magni, etc.) or Growth. This limits her synergy with defense-boosting tanks.
fits her perfectly. Since her base damage is on the lower side, the extra kick helps her hit closer to a true sniper level, especially on that first fast charge.

Upon release in March 2025, Leonie was a topic of heated debate. Her damage was seen as "meh," but her revive was considered broken. She quickly found a home on War Defense teams designed to time-out opponents.
As we head into 2026, her stats are no longer elite. Hit-3 heroes now regularly deal 350-400% unconditional damage. However,
no other hero revives as reliably or with as much resources (HP/Mana) as she does.
She is no longer a top-tier nuker, but she remains a top-tier

. In Bloody Battle tournaments or Rush wars, her revive mechanic can single-handedly win games by exhausting the enemy. She is less effective in standard raiding where newer, harder-hitting heroes end fights before her bleed can ramp up.
Leonie is a Rogue, which gives her a chance to Dodge direct damage. This synergy with her Family Bonus is excellent.
Maximizes the value of her "Nine Lives." Every time she revives with 50% HP, that HP pool is larger. The longer she lives, the more bleeds she stacks.
Her direct hit remains soft against non-minion enemies.
Pushes her Attack stat to modern levels (~1450+), making her hit sting significantly more and maximizing the bleed damage (if it scaled, but Surge Bleed is fixed).
Correction: Surge Bleed is fixed damage, so Attack path only buffs the direct hit.
She dies faster, potentially reducing the number of revive loops.
path is recommended. Her primary role is to stay on the board, apply pressure via DoT, and waste the opponent's flags with her revive loops.
The 80% revive chance with high Mana is the best revive mechanic in the game.
Punishes minion metas (Waterpipe, etc.) severely with massive damage boosts.
Her DoT ignores high defense and defensive buffs.
Immune to Defense Down means she never gets "softened up" for a kill shot.
Without minions to target, 250% is weak for a 5-star.
Her damage relies heavily on the Surge Bleed, which can be cleansed.
Lower defense makes her prone to being one-shot by modern snipers, forcing reliance on the revive RNG.
If the 80% roll fails on the first death, she offers very little value.
A monster. She revives, you can't heal. Her bleed ticks down your health while you struggle to keep her dead.
Excellent flank or wing. She punishes bad boards by coming back to life when the attacker thinks they've won.
Firing constantly allows her to ramp up the Surge Bleed to its 3000 damage cap quickly.
Leonie works best in "Attrition" or "Stall" teams. She doesn't burst enemies down; she wears them out.
Alby - Leonie - Phorcys - Tyr - Mother North
. A revive-heavy team. Just when you kill one, another brings them back, or they revive themselves.
. Grimble destroys minions to charge mana, Leonie and Ogima follow up with massive hits to the exposed heroes.
While her passive says it can't be prevented by status ailments, certain hard-coded "Perma-Death" mechanics (if introduced) would stop her.
Note: Kravekrush does NOT stop her passive.
Heroes that disable passives might eventually be a counter, though currently rare.
Rigard, Ariel, etc., remove her bleed, neutering her threat.
Do not pair her with heroes whose main utility is Defense Up (e.g., Vivica), as she won't benefit.
If you pulled Leonie, level her immediately. Her revive mechanic is a safety net that will help you complete difficult map stages and quests even if your healers die. She is forgiving of mistakes.

Leonie is a niche tool for you. She is not your primary raid attacker. Use her on Defense to annoy attackers or in specific War situations (Minion wars). Do not limit break her twice (LB2) unless you absolutely love the "Undying" playstyle; there are better stats to chase.
The community reaction to Leonie was a mix of awe and frustration.
"I can see it now: in the upcoming war, they’ll pair these Heroes with revive-focused toons... I’m excited for it!"
- A player anticipating the stall meta.
"9 lives… yeah… if you have the hero, the probability will be 5% revive. If you face it at raid, the probability will be around 100%."
"She is the prize from this portal... not looking forward to facing that ‘nine lives’ passive on Defense."
The consensus remains: She is the ultimate "Rage Quit" generator.

Leonie is the cat that got the cream, ate it, died, and came back for seconds. While her claws might not be as sharp as the newest legendary heroes, her ability to defy death makes her timelessly useful. She turns straightforward battles into chaotic gambles, often tilting the odds in your favor simply by refusing to leave the battlefield.
A solid, annoying, and unique hero that will age better than pure damage dealers thanks to her mechanics.
Is she worth the mats? Yes, especially if you enjoy making your war opponents throw their phones.
A detailed review of Leonie, the 5-star Nature hero from the Wild Cat family. Analysis of her Nine Lives passive, surge bleed mechanics, and strategy.
